note: Scoped review by fresh maker-not-checker agents; 2 confirmed (both major), both fixed and verified by a green battery (250 tests). Rejected findings were adversarially refuted. Security: keyless server-side fetch, no secrets; full sweep at G7.
fixedFindings: mapForecast over-strictness: now requires only CORE fields (date, hi/lo, weather code); precip/uv/wind/cloud/feels are number|null so an Open-Meteo horizon-null never drops a day or fails the forecast — DayCard shows a calm placeholder, comfortScore uses neutral defaults (regression test added)

spec contradiction (app-shell vs forecast on invalid deep-link coords) reconciled by amending the forecast spec: malformed/out-of-range deep links are handled by the app-shell empty-state Notice (FR-SHELL-03 — search is the right recovery, not retry); fetchForecast keeps coord validation as defense-in-depth; forecast calm-error+retry covers valid-coord fetch/parse failures. The implementation already did the correct thing; the spec was amended to match.
